cases.title: Avery v. Nashville Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC declared Case 2016-06 to have been abandoned and approved this action without objection.

cases.title: In re Korean Northwest Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Korean Northwest Presbytery amended and adopted its responses to SJC exceptions at its April 2017 meeting; the SJC accepted those amended and corrected responses by roll call vote (18 concur, 1 dissent, 1 abstain).

cases.title: Frazier v. Nashville Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Frazier complained that Nashville Presbytery erred in finding insufficient evidence to raise a strong presumption of guilt against TE Scott Sauls regarding his teaching on same-sex attraction; the SJC denied the complaint 20-0-1, concluding NP fulfilled its BCO 31-2 investigatory duties, accompanied by several concurring opinions raising procedural and exegetical concerns.

cases.title: Daniels et al. v. Nashville Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC declared the complaint moot by a vote of 13-6; the substantial dissenting opinion by six members argued the Commission provided no rationale for the mootness determination and that no constitutional basis for declaring it moot was established.

cases.title: Bachmann v. Nashville Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Bachmann appealed Nashville Presbytery's decision to amend its judicial commission's recommended censure of admonition to indefinite suspension; the SJC sustained the appeal, finding NP violated BCO 15-3 by dividing the question and amending the JC's judgment, and rendered the decision that should have been rendered by restoring the JC's censure of admonition.
